title: Day 05 — Dashboard Layout with React Router
summary: Learn SPA routing with React Router — nested layouts, Link, Outlet, and navigation — by building a dashboard with sidebar, navbar, profile, settings, and 404.

blocks.content: Your GitHub search and login live on one screen. Real products — Jira, Linear, Vercel — have dozens of pages sharing one shell. Today you add React Router and build a dashboard layout where sidebar and navbar persist while the main content swaps. URLs become shareable. Back button works. This is the architecture behind every SaaS admin panel.

blocks.title: Scope for Day 05
blocks.content: Use React Router, Link, Outlet, and nested routes. Custom hooks and debounce come tomorrow on Day 06. Do not introduce Redux or Context yet.

Each route renders components that attach event handlers. Those handlers are closures — they capture variables from the render where they were created. When ProfilePage defines onClick={() => navigate('/settings')}, it closes over navigate from that render. Closures are not router-specific but explain handler behavior across navigations.

When routes include parameters like /users/:id, useParams returns a typed object. Define a Params type or use generics from React Router. Today your routes may be static — still type NavItem config arrays.
A Single Page Application loads index.html once. JavaScript changes the view when the URL changes. No white flash. No re-downloading CSS. React Router connects URL paths to component trees.

rows: Enables routing context for the app
Link with active style when route matches
Renders nested child route in layout

blocks.title: Outlet is required
blocks.content: If URL changes but content does not, you almost certainly forgot Outlet in the layout or nested routes incorrectly.

blocks.title: NavLink active class
blocks.content: NavLink accepts className={({ isActive }) => isActive ? 'active' : ''} for sidebar highlight — interview-visible polish.

items.text: Sidebar with links to dashboard, profile, settings
Navbar with app title or user area

blocks.content: Symptom: blank page or router warning. Cause: path typo or missing element. Fix: match Link to with Route path; use React Router devtools or console.
Symptom: URL updates, content stuck. Cause: missing Outlet or flat routes instead of nested. Fix: nest child routes under layout Route with Outlet.
